articles.title: Peace Gets an Extra 3 Weeks! Israel and Lebanon Agree to Extend Ceasefire
articles.summary: Imagine two kids in a fight finally agreeing to stop—then deciding to keep the peace for a little longer. That's what happened between Israel and Lebanon! President Trump announced they agreed to extend a ceasefire (a pause in fighting) by three weeks. The first 10-day ceasefire was about to end, but after a meeting at the White House, the two countries said yes to more time. The goal is to stop attacks, pull Israeli troops out of Lebanon, and start rebuilding. But there's a tricky part: a group called Hezbollah says it won't follow the agreement. And there have already been some rule-breakers on both sides. A journalist was even killed. Still, many hope this extra time will lead to…

articles.title: Mind Reader at the White House? Oz Pearlman to Perform at Big Dinner
articles.summary: What if someone could read Donald Trump's mind—right in front of a room full of journalists? That's exactly what mentalist Oz Pearlman plans to do at the White House Correspondents' Dinner this Saturday! A mentalist is like a magician, but instead of pulling rabbits out of hats, they seem to pull thoughts out of people's heads. Pearlman went viral for guessing people's PIN numbers and secrets on TV. Now, instead of a comedian roasting politicians, he'll be the star performer. President Trump will be there, and Pearlman hints that Trump will be part of the act. "Reading Donald Trump's mind is arguably the most impressive thing you could ever do," he says. It's a big change from tradition, but everyone's…

articles.title: Professors Sue Their Own University Over Protest Arrests
articles.summary: Three professors at Emory University in Atlanta are suing their own school! They say the university broke its own rules when it called police to break up a pro-Palestine protest in 2024. The professors say they were just watching or trying to help when they were arrested. One professor was thrown face-first to the ground and got a concussion (a brain injury) and a spine injury. Another was charged just for yelling "Stop!" at an officer. None of them were convicted of anything. Now they want the university to pay back the money they spent defending themselves. The university says the lawsuit is "without merit" and that they acted to keep everyone safe. This is part of a bigger debate…
